children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established a national cooperative geologic mapping program between the United States Geological Survey and the State geological surveys, acting through the Association.

children.label: A
children.text: designed and administered to achieve the objectives set forth in subsection (c);

children.label: B
children.text: developed in consultation with the advisory committee; and

children.label: C
children.text: administered through the Survey.

children.label: A
children.text: develop a 5-year strategic plan for the geologic mapping program in accordance with , which plan shall be submitted to the Committee on Resources of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate not later than 1 year after ;section 31e of this titleMarch 12, 2019

children.label: B
children.text: appoint, with the advice and consultation of the Association, the advisory committee not later than 1 year after , in accordance with ; andMarch 12, 2019section 31d of this title

children.label: i
children.text: how the Survey and the Association are coordinating the development and implementation of the geologic mapping program;

children.label: ii
children.text: how the Survey and the Association establish goals, mapping priorities, and target dates for implementation of the geologic mapping program; and

children.label: iii
children.text: how long-term staffing plans for the various components of the geologic mapping program affect successful implementation of the geologic mapping program.

children.label: A
children.text: in cooperation with the Association, other Federal and State agencies, public and private sector organizations and academia, the geologic-map data base; and

children.label: B
children.text: maps and mapping techniques which achieve the objectives specified in subsection (c).

determining the Nation’s geologic framework through systematic development of geologic maps at scales appropriate to the geologic setting and the perceived applications, such maps to be contributed to the national geologic map data base;
development of a complementary national geochronologic and paleontologic data base that provides value-added descriptive and interpretative information to the geologic-map data base;

application of cost-effective mapping techniques that assemble, produce, translate and disseminate geologic-map information and that render such information of greater application and benefit to the public; and
development of public awareness of the role and application of geologic-map information to the resolution of national issues of land use management.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The geologic mapping program shall include a Federal geologic mapping component, the objective of which shall be to determine the geologic framework of areas determined to be vital to the economic, social, environmental, or scientific welfare of the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be described in the 5-year plan under ; andsection 31e of this title

children.label: I
children.text: national requirements for geologic map information in areas of multiple-issue need or areas of compelling single-issue need;

children.label: II
children.text: national requirements for geologic map information in areas where mapping is required to solve critical earth science problems; and

children.label: III
children.text: the needs of land management agencies of the Department of the Interior.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Federal component shall include interdisciplinary studies that add value to geologic mapping.

children.label: I
children.text: establishment of a national geologic map database under ;section 31f of this title

children.label: II
children.text: studies that lead to the implementation of cost-effective digital methods for the acquisition, compilation, analysis, cartographic production, and dissemination of geologic map information;

children.label: III
children.text: paleontologic, geochrono-logic, and isotopic investigations that provide information critical to understanding the age and history of geologic map units;

children.label: IV
children.text: geophysical investigations that assist in delineating and mapping the physical characteristics and 3-dimensional distribution of geologic materials and geologic structures; and

children.label: V
children.text: geochemical investigations and analytical operations that characterize the composition of geologic map units.

children.heading: Use of results
children.label: iii Use of results
children.text: The results of investigations under clause (ii) shall be contributed to national databases.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The geologic mapping program shall include a State geologic mapping component, the objective of which shall be to establish the geologic framework of areas determined to be vital to the economic, social, environmental, or scientific welfare of individual States.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be determined by State panels representing a broad range of users of geologic maps; and

children.label: I
children.text: State requirements for geologic map information in areas of multiple-issue need or areas of compelling single-issue need; and

children.label: II
children.text: State requirements for geologic map information in areas where mapping is required to solve critical earth science problems.

children.heading: Integration of Federal and State priorities
children.label: C Integration of Federal and State priorities
children.text: A national panel including representatives of the Survey shall integrate the State mapping priorities under this paragraph with the Federal mapping priorities under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: D Use of funds
children.text: The Survey and recipients of grants under the State component shall not use more than 15.25 percent of the Federal funds made available under the State component for any fiscal year to pay indirect, servicing, or program management charges.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: E Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of activities under the State component for any fiscal year shall not exceed 50 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: to provide for broad education in geologic mapping and field analysis through support of field studies; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to develop academic programs that teach students of earth science the fundamental principles of geologic mapping and field analysis.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be integrated with the Federal component and the State component; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall respond to mapping priorities identified for the Federal component and the State component.

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: C Use of funds
children.text: The Survey and recipients of grants under the education component shall not use more than 15.25 percent of the Federal funds made available under the education component for any fiscal year to pay indirect, servicing, or program management charges.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: D Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of activities under the education component for any fiscal year shall not exceed 50 percent.

children.heading: Abandoned mine land and mine waste component
children.label: 4 Abandoned mine land and mine waste component

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The geologic mapping program shall include an abandoned mine land and mine waste geologic mapping component, the objective of which shall be to establish the geologic framework of abandoned mine land and other land containing mine waste.

children.heading: Mapping priorities
children.label: B Mapping priorities
children.text: For the component described in subparagraph (A), the priority shall be mapping abandoned mine land and other land containing mine waste where multiple critical mineral (as defined in ) and metal commodities are anticipated to be present, rather than single mineral resources.section 1606(a) of title 30
